title: Plugin chain P3: runtime dependency packaging (ajv/ajv-formats/gray-matter resolution at the user's machine)
description:
Third ticket of the plugin packaging chain (epic TASK-020). PROBLEM: the plugin's standalone Node entrypoints (`bin/init.js`, `bin/new-task.js`, and the future MCP server) import `ajv`/`ajv-formats` (src/task-store.js) and `gray-matter` (src/knowledge.js). After a real (git-URL) plugin install, no `node_modules` ships and ESM `import` ignores `NODE_PATH` — so these deps would not resolve on the user's machine.

**APPROACH (human-decided 2026-05-29, superseding the earlier data-dir/NODE_PATH lock — see comments): esbuild bundling.** Bundle each standalone entrypoint into a self-contained committed artifact with ALL deps (src/ modules + ajv + ajv-formats + gray-matter) inlined. Runtime resolution becomes a non-issue: `node dist/init.cjs` carries everything. This is robust against every harness quirk we hit (git-clone strips node_modules; ESM ignores NODE_PATH; plugin-root is ephemeral) and is the pattern Anthropic's own agent-sdk-dev plugin uses.

**Consequences of this approach:**
- NO SessionStart hook, NO NODE_PATH, NO data-dir install. The CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR robustness folded in from TASK-022 is therefore MOOT — `resolveRepoRoot`'s cwd fallback (shipped in TASK-022) is empirically sufficient for the shipped invocation paths; document that and do not add a hook.
- `ajv`/`ajv-formats`/`gray-matter` can STAY in `devDependencies` (needed only for `npm test` against src/ and for the build-time bundle; never resolved at runtime). No dependency-section move required.
- The build output dir MUST be committed (NOT gitignored). The repo's `.gitignore` currently ignores `dist/` — either remove that entry or emit to a committed dir (e.g. `dist/` un-ignored, or `bundles/`). The `.gitattributes` LF rule (committed in dc9b5d1) keeps the bundled shebang valid.

Scope:
1. Add `esbuild` as a devDependency + an `npm run build:plugin` script that bundles `bin/init.js` and `bin/new-task.js` (the MCP server bundle is deferred to P6) into self-contained `.cjs` files (`--bundle --platform=node --format=cjs`, with a `#!/usr/bin/env node` banner).
2. Wire the plugin's shipped entrypoints to the bundles: update `.claude-plugin/shipped-bin.json` (and the bin exposure) so the installed plugin runs the committed bundles, while `bin/*.js` + `src/*` remain the dev/test sources (npm test unchanged, runs against src/).
3. Commit the bundle artifacts; ensure they are not gitignored.

**The tests-first specs committed in bd1ac0e (tests/plugin-deps.spec.js) target the ABANDONED data-dir/hook approach and MUST be rewritten for esbuild before impl.**
acceptance_criteria: An `npm run build:plugin` script exists (esbuild as devDependency) that bundles `bin/init.js` and `bin/new-task.js` into self-contained committed `.cjs` artifacts with a `#!/usr/bin/env node` banner. Running it is reproducible and deterministic.

RUNTIME RESOLUTION PROOF: a test copies a built bundle (e.g. `dist/init.cjs`) into a temp dir with NO `node_modules` anywhere up-tree and runs `node <bundle>` (or `node --check`), asserting it loads/parses with zero MODULE_NOT_FOUND — proving deps are inlined, not resolved.
The build output is committed and NOT gitignored (remove `dist/` from `.gitignore` or emit to a committed dir); a check confirms the bundle files are tracked by git.

The plugin's shipped entrypoints (`.claude-plugin/shipped-bin.json` + bin exposure) point at the committed bundles, not the raw `bin/*.js`; `bin/*.js` + `src/*` remain the dev sources and `npm test` still runs against src/ unchanged.

No SessionStart hook / NODE_PATH is introduced; a comment documents that the TASK-022 `resolveRepoRoot` cwd fallback satisfies project-root resolution for the bundled entrypoints (CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR robustness moot under bundling).
Full suite stays green (the rewritten tests-first specs pass post-impl; the abandoned bd1ac0e hook/planSetup specs are removed/replaced); zero regressions in the pre-existing suite.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Minted from TASK-020 spike §F (P3) + §B.4. This is the packaging bug the audit surfaced (AC15 of the §B.2 table). Gates both P4 (init runs task-store on the user machine) and P6 (MCP server imports task-store).

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: FOLDED IN from the TASK-022 review: P3 already builds a SessionStart hook (for the ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} first-run npm install + NODE_PATH wiring). The reviewer recommended the belt-and-suspenders `export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` robustness (deferred from P2) ride along on THAT same SessionStart hook rather than building a separate half-hook in P2. So: when you author the SessionStart hook here, also have it export/derive CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR so bin/MCP subprocesses inherit the project root even if a command's cwd is not the project root. The cwd fallback (resolveRepoRoot, shipped in TASK-022) remains the primary mechanism; this hook is the robustness backstop. Add it as an extra AC/behavior, with a test.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: APPROACH LOCKED after a claude-code-guide research pass + an empirical install probe. CHOICE: the documented `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` first-run-install pattern via a SessionStart hook (NOT vendoring, NOT esbuild). Grounded facts: (1) `plugin install` from a GIT-URL marketplace git-clones the repo, so gitignored `node_modules` is NOT shipped — committed deps would be required to vendor. (Empirical note: a LOCAL-path marketplace file-copies the working tree and DID drag node_modules along, but that's a dev-only false friend; real distribution is git-based.) (2) A SessionStart hook CAN export env vars to ALL subsequent Bash-tool subprocesses + MCP + bin via `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` (confirmed) — so one hook handles BOTH `NODE_PATH=${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/node_modules` AND the folded-in `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` export. (3) Vendoring rejected: committing node_modules bloats the repo with hundreds of files. (4) esbuild-bundle rejected for now: introduces a build toolchain to a project that's currently plain-ESM-no-build. Audience is Claude Code devs (have npm), so first-run `npm install` latency is acceptable; revisit esbuild/vendoring only if first-run latency or offline-install becomes a requirement. Cite: docs hooks.md (CLAUDE_ENV_FILE), plugins-reference (${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}). IMPLEMENT: deps→dependencies; hooks/hooks.json SessionStart → a hook script that (a) diffs bundled package.json vs a stored copy in ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} and npm-installs there on first run/change, (b) writes NODE_PATH + CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR exports to $CLAUDE_ENV_FILE; .mcp.json (when P6 lands) also sets NODE_PATH in its env block.

comments.author: developer
comments.body: TESTS-FIRST attempt 1 (commit bd1ac0e) surfaced a BLOCKER that invalidates the locked data-dir/NODE_PATH approach: ESM `import` IGNORES NODE_PATH (CommonJS-only; empirically confirmed on Node v22.19.0). src/task-store.js + src/knowledge.js use ESM `import` of bare specifiers, so `export NODE_PATH=${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/node_modules` will NOT make them resolve — deps must be reachable up-tree from the importing file, OR inlined. Encoded as the control test `control_NODE_PATH_alone_does_NOT_resolve_ESM_imports`. Also flagged a pre-existing suite breakage (24 specs) from CRLF shebangs in bin/*.js — orchestrator root-caused + fixed via .gitattributes (commit dc9b5d1).

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: APPROACH PIVOTED (human decision, supersedes the 01:10 lock): esbuild BUNDLE, not data-dir/NODE_PATH. The ESM-ignores-NODE_PATH finding made the data-dir approach require a fragile junction-into-the-ephemeral-cache-dir hack; bundling inlines deps so there is NO runtime resolution at all. Rejected: vendoring (commits hundreds of node_modules files) and the data-dir+junction (most moving parts, first-run npm/network, cross-platform junction). Consequences captured in the rewritten ACs: deps stay devDependencies; NO SessionStart hook; CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR robustness is MOOT (resolveRepoRoot cwd fallback suffices); build output must be committed (un-ignore dist/). The bd1ac0e tests-first specs (planSetup/hooks.json) are now wrong-target and must be rewritten for esbuild before impl. The .gitattributes LF fix (dc9b5d1) keeps the bundled .cjs shebang valid.

comments.author: developer
comments.body: IMPL complete via esbuild bundling. Commits: 37f313a (tests-first esbuild rewrite), 9845792 (impl), f456114 (orchestrator-authorized shipped-bin reconciliation). Added esbuild devDep + scripts/build-plugin.mjs (`npm run build:plugin`) bundling bin/init.js + bin/new-task.js → dist/init.cjs (348kb) + dist/new-task.cjs (306kb), reproducible byte-identical. Solved import.meta.url by INLINING the JSON schemas: src/task-store.js + src/project-md.js switched from readFileSync(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)) to `import … with { type: 'json' }` (verified resolving under Vite/vitest, raw Node ESM, and esbuild; project-md normalizes the imported object to preserve the graceful-degrade .properties contract). Added a bundling-aware run-as-main guard to both bin shells (import.meta.url check when truthy, else require.main===module) so the cjs bundle actually runs. .gitignore un-ignores dist/; both bundles git-tracked. shipped-bin.json → [dist/init.cjs, dist/new-task.cjs]. NO SessionStart hook; deps stay devDependencies. 263 passed / 1 skipped, deterministic.

comments.author: reviewer
comments.body: Verdict (fresh context): APPROVE. 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 0 LOW / 1 NIT. 263 passed + 1 skipped, twice, deterministic; pre-existing 237+1 green (zero regressions). Tests-first confirmed: git show 9845792 -- tests/ empty; the sole authorized test edit (f456114) touches only plugin-scaffold.spec.js AC4 + shipped-bin.json, documented. INDEPENDENTLY rebuilt via build:plugin → byte-identical to committed bundles; copied committed dist/*.cjs to a tmp dir with no node_modules up-tree + unset NODE_PATH → both run to arg-parse with ZERO MODULE_NOT_FOUND; bundle scan confirms fileURLToPath count 0, bare require('ajv'|…) count 0, schema literals inlined. High-risk src/ refactor (task-store.js + project-md.js JSON-import swap) verified behavior-preserving — task-store-hardening AC5 + project-md-hardening AC2 specs green; graceful-degrade .properties contract preserved/hardened. bin run-as-main guard correct (no auto-run on import/require; resolveRepoRoot binding untouched). Authorized TASK-021 AC4 edit judged legitimate staleness fix, intent preserved, make-template exclusion now stricter + still-physically-present guard green. All 6 ACs COVERED. NIT: build-plugin.mjs esbuild opts duplicated in the in-test helper (non-drift today — byte-identical rebuild confirms). Nothing blocks close.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Closed on reviewer APPROVE under standing auto-close authorization. Linked commits 37f313a (test) + 9845792 (impl) + f456114 (authorized reconciliation). Context: the chain-blocking CRLF/shebang infra bug fixed mid-ticket via .gitattributes (dc9b5d1). The hard ticket of the chain — two approach pivots (data-dir/NODE_PATH → esbuild), an infra bug, and a cross-ticket AC4 conflict — all resolved. NIT (esbuild-opts duplication between build script and test helper) deferred without follow-up; byte-identical rebuild covers it today. Proceeding to TASK-024 (P4 /init-project command).
